stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 4 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability between 63% and 77% (uncertain due to limited operational data). sensor 2 has a reliability of 97%. sensor 3 has a reliability of 85%. sensor 4 has a reliability of 78%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all sensors simultaneously, estimated between 2.2% and 4.8%. What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9516 to 0.9778. Point estimate: 0.9647.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 4 sensors fail simultaneously).
With common-cause failure (2.2%-4.8%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9516, 0.9778].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
